params.description: Hundred Grid Mode B appears with canonical 4 × 5 rectangle filled. 20 square tiles visible. Build affordances disabled — student sees rectangle but cannot place tiles.

beats.text: Here is the rectangle you built last lesson. It has 4 square tiles in each row and 5 rows. The area is 20 square tiles.

validator.description: Student tapped Check, Mode C activates

elements.description: Hundred Grid Mode B with 4 × 5 rectangle. Check button active.

params.description: Mode C activates. Two-panel strip appears below rectangle: left panel shows 4 × 5 = 20, right panel shows 20 = 4 × 5. Bridge element (double-headed arrow or =) between panels signals equivalence. Both panels identical font size, background, visual weight.
Static balance-scale asset appears in tutor explanation zone alongside Mode C strip. Shows balance beam with equal weight on both sides.

beats.text: Look at the equal sign. It is not a go button. It is a balance. What is on this side has the same value as what is on that side. The equal sign means both sides have the same value.

elements.description: Hundred Grid Mode C active. Rectangle visible above. Two-panel strip below: left panel 4 × 5 = 20, right panel 20 = 4 × 5, bridge element between. Tap-and-hold enabled on panels.
Balance-scale asset visible in tutor zone.

beats.text: Tap and hold each panel. See what happens.
Tap and hold the left panel, then the right panel.

validator.description: Student tapped and held both panels, observed rectangle highlighting

params.description: Both panels cross-highlight rectangle when tapped. Same rectangle highlights for both equations.

beats.text: Both equations point to the same rectangle. They have the same value.

elements.description: Hundred Grid Mode C. Rectangle visible. Two-panel strip: left 4 × 5 = 20, right 20 = 4 × 5. Tap-and-hold complete, both panels confirmed.
Balance-scale asset remains visible in tutor zone.

options: both sides have the same value
the answer goes on the right
params.description: Fill-in-the-blank stem appears: The equal sign means ___. Three options visible.

beats.text: Now finish the sentence. The equal sign means what?

condition.selected: both sides have the same value

validator.description: Student selected both sides have the same value, correct

beats.text: You got it. The equal sign means both sides have the same value. It is a balance, not a go button.

elements.description: Hundred Grid Mode C. Rectangle visible. Two-panel strip: left 4 × 5 = 20, right 20 = 4 × 5.
Balance-scale asset visible in tutor zone.
Fill-in-the-blank stem completed. Correct option both sides have the same value selected.
elements.selected: both sides have the same value
